req.title: Create observation
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates three distinct test paths: (1) a valid LOINC-coded Observation POST that succeeds (EP positive partition), (2) a POST without a code element that must return 422 (NEG / DT error row), and (3) a boundary case where the code object is present but its coding array is empty — the minimum structure that could satisfy or violate the LOINC constraint (BVA lower-bound). Each test uses a dedicated clinician-scoped session fixture and a pre-created patient fixture to satisfy preconditions, keeping the tests idempotent by relying on server-assigned IDs and asserting on response state rather than persisted state.

tests.name: test_create_observation_with_valid_loinc_code
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as role:clinician via <clinician_auth_headers> fixture
A patient resource exists; its ID is supplied by <existing_patient_id> fixture
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Construct an Observation payload with resourceType, status, code containing a LOINC coding (system=http://loinc.org, code=8867-4), and subject referencing the patient
POST payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
expected: Response body contains an id field (server-assigned)
trace: R-HLT-005: clinician shall CREATE an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element

tests.name: test_create_observation_missing_code_returns_422
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as role:clinician via <clinician_auth_headers> fixture
A patient resource exists; its ID is supplied by <existing_patient_id> fixture
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Construct an Observation payload with resourceType and status but NO code field
POST payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
expected: Response body indicates validation failure related to missing code element
trace: R-HLT-005: Observations without code shall be rejected
R-HLT-005: error pathway 422 when code missing

tests.name: test_create_observation_empty_coding_array_rejected
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as role:clinician via <clinician_auth_headers> fixture
A patient resource exists; its ID is supplied by <existing_patient_id> fixture
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Construct an Observation payload where code is present as an object but its coding array is empty (zero elements — lower boundary of the coding array cardinality)
POST payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
expected: HTTP status code is 422 (empty coding array cannot satisfy LOINC-coded requirement)
Response body indicates that at least one coding entry with a valid LOINC system is required
trace: R-HLT-005: LOINC-coded code element is mandatory; zero codings is the boundary below the valid minimum of 1
R-HLT-005: error pathway 422 when code missing (structurally present but semantically empty)

tests.name: test_create_observation_non_loinc_system_rejected
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as role:clinician via <clinician_auth_headers> fixture
A patient resource exists; its ID is supplied by <existing_patient_id> fixture
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: Construct an Observation payload with a coding entry using a SNOMED system (http://snomed.info/sct) instead of LOINC
POST payload to /Observation with clinician auth headers
expected: Response body indicates that a LOINC-coded code element is required
trace: R-HLT-005: code element must be LOINC-coded; non-LOINC system is a distinct decision-table row (code present, wrong system)
R-HLT-005: error pathway — invalid code system triggers rejection
